SEARCH
Package de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.solvertypes

Class SolverTypeImplementation

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.solvertypes.SolverTypeImplementation
All Implemented Interfaces:
SolverType
public final class SolverTypeImplementation extends Object implements SolverType
Final SolverType implementation that uses building blocks for the various methods. This can be used to modify a ModifiableSolverType object using .props files only, for example (see SolverPropertiesLoader). The building blocks that can be modified on creation are: - The various String parameters such as default command, params and the name - The solver type's default timeout - The message delimiters used by solver processes of the created solver type - The AbstractSolverSocket used to interpret messages by solver processes of the created type - The SMTTranslator used to translate messages for processes of the created solver type - The SMTHandlers used by the translator (if ModularSMTLib2Translator is used) - The preamble file used for translations for the solver type

  • Constructor Details

    • SolverTypeImplementation

      public SolverTypeImplementation(String name, String info, String defaultParams, String defaultCommand, String versionParameter, String minimumSupportedVersion, long defaultTimeout, String[] delimiters, Class<?> translatorClass, String[] handlerNames, String[] handlerOptions, Class<?> solverSocketClass, @Nullable String preamble)
      Instantiate the solver type object with all its default values. The changeable values such as command and params are initially the same as their default values.
      Parameters:
      name - the name, e.g. "Z3"
      info - some information about the solver type
      defaultParams - the default command line PARAMETERS used to start the actual solver program
      defaultCommand - the default command line COMMAND used to start the actual solver program
      versionParameter - the command line parameter used to get the versionParameter of the actual solver program
      minimumSupportedVersion - the minimum supported versionParameter of the solver type at hand
      defaultTimeout - the default solver timeout for SMT processes using this solver type
      delimiters - the message delimiters used by the actual solver program
      translatorClass - the SMTTranslator class used by this solver type
      handlerNames - the names of the SMTHandlers to be used by the SMTTranslator created by this solver type
      handlerOptions - arbitrary String options used by the SMTHandlers
      solverSocketClass - the AbstractSolverSocket class used by the solver type at hand
      preamble - the preamble String for the created SMTTranslator, may be null

  • Method Details

    • isInstalled

      public static boolean isInstalled(@Nullable String cmd)
      Returns false whenever cmd is null or empty, otherwise if the command is an absolute path it is checked, if not the environment variables are checked for the command whether there is a file with the command's name in any of those paths. If all of these fail, the cmd is also not installed.
      Parameters:
      cmd - the command whose existence will be checked
      Returns:
      true iff the command is a non-empty String and a file with the command's name or with the command as pathname can be found in the file system.

    • createSolver

      public SMTSolver createSolver(SMTProblem problem, SolverListener listener, Services services)
      Description copied from interface: SolverType
      Creates an instance of SMTSolver representing a concrete instance of that solver.
      Specified by:
      createSolver in interface SolverType
      Parameters:
      problem - the SMT problem that is sent to the resulting SMT solver process
      listener - the listener for the solver process
      services - services object to be used by the solver process, if needed (see for example SMTObjTranslator)
      Returns:
      a concrete solver process of the type at hand

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Specified by:
      getName in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      the name of the solver.

    • isInstalled

      public boolean isInstalled(boolean recheck)
      Description copied from interface: SolverType
      Checks whether the solver is installed. If recheck is set to true the method should check for the path variable of the system and for the absolute path, otherwise it can return the result of the previous call.
      Specified by:
      isInstalled in interface SolverType
      Parameters:
      recheck - if this is true, the installation check will be done again instead of returning the result from the previous call of this method
      Returns:
      whether the solver is installed (i.e. the command is an existing file)

    • getInfo

      public String getInfo()
      Description copied from interface: SolverType
      Some specific information about the solver which can be presented. null means no information.
      Specified by:
      getInfo in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      the solver-type-specific information, null if there is none

    • getSolverParameters

      public String getSolverParameters()
      Description copied from interface: SolverType
      Unless SolverType.setSolverParameters(String) is called, these should be the same as SolverType.getDefaultSolverParameters().
      Specified by:
      getSolverParameters in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      the currently used parameters for the solver.

      setSolverParameters in interface SolverType
      Parameters:

    • getDefaultSolverParameters

      public String getDefaultSolverParameters()
      Specified by:
      getDefaultSolverParameters in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      the default parameters which should be used for starting a solver

    • getSolverCommand

      public String getSolverCommand()
      Description copied from interface: SolverType
      The currently used command for starting the solver. For example "z3" if it is registered in the PATH variable, otherwise "ABSOLUTE_PATH/z3". Unless SolverType.setSolverCommand(String) has been called, this should be the same as SolverType.getDefaultSolverCommand().
      Specified by:
      getSolverCommand in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      the currently used solver command for starting solver processes

    • setSolverCommand

      public void setSolverCommand(String s)
      Description copied from interface: SolverType
      Set the solver command to be used for starting processes with the type at hand.
      Specified by:
      setSolverCommand in interface SolverType
      Parameters:
      s - the new solver command

    • getDefaultSolverCommand

      public String getDefaultSolverCommand()
      Specified by:
      getDefaultSolverCommand in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      the default solver command for processes of the type at hand

    • getSolverTimeout

      public long getSolverTimeout()
      Description copied from interface: SolverType
      Unless SolverType.setSolverTimeout(long) has been called, this should be the same as SolverType.getDefaultSolverTimeout().
      Specified by:
      getSolverTimeout in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      the currently set solver timeout, e.g. 2000L milliseconds

      public SMTTranslator createTranslator()
      Specified by:

      Returns:
      the translator to be used. So far each solver supports only one format.

    • getDelimiters

      public String[] getDelimiters()
      Description copied from interface: SolverType
      Message delimiters used by the solver to separate its messages sent to KeY. For example, those could be "\n".
      Specified by:
      getDelimiters in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      the delimiters of the messages that are sent from the solver to KeY.

      modifyProblem in interface SolverType
      Parameters:
      problem - the SMT problem to modify
      Returns:
      the modified SMT problem String

      Returns:

      Returns:

    • getInstalledVersion

      public String getInstalledVersion()
      Specified by:
      getInstalledVersion in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      the current version that is installed, if it has already been checked, otherwise null

    • getRawVersion

      public @Nullable String getRawVersion()
      Description copied from interface: SolverType
      Retrieve the version string without check for support. Returns null if the solver is not installed.
      Specified by:
      getRawVersion in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      the installed version of the type at hand, if it is installed - null otherwise

    • isSupportedVersion

      public boolean isSupportedVersion()
      Specified by:
      isSupportedVersion in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      whether the currently installed version is supported.

    • checkForSupport

      public boolean checkForSupport()
      Note that the actual version is only compared to the minimum version lexicographically. This is a temporary solution as it may lead to weird behaviour, for example shorter Strings will be before longer Strings and 1.14.1 will be before 1.8.10 even though they have the same length. If that lexicographical comparison is not possible, you may have to modify the SolverTypeImplementation class and change SolverPropertiesLoader accordingly. TODO Find better solution
      Specified by:
      checkForSupport in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      true iff the solver type is installed and its version is supported

    • supportHasBeenChecked

      public boolean supportHasBeenChecked()
      Specified by:
      supportHasBeenChecked in interface SolverType
      Returns:
      true iff the support has been checked for the currently installed solver.

    • getSocket

      public @NonNull AbstractSolverSocket getSocket(ModelExtractor query)
      Description copied from interface: SolverType
      Creates a new solver socket that can handle the communication for the given solver type.
      Specified by:
      getSocket in interface SolverType
      Parameters:
      query - the ModelExtractor that can be used to extract a counterexample (for non-CE solvers this can be null)
      Returns:
      the newly created socket
